signal_title: DOJ's Final Rule Implementing Executive Order 14117
risk_rationale: The DOJ's Final Rule aims to prevent foreign adversaries from accessing sensitive data, which directly impacts TikTok's data sovereignty and localization policies, particularly concerning US user data. This introduces a material compliance gap that requires immediate product changes to ensure compliance with the new due diligence and reporting requirements.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Employee access to US data is restricted to US-based personnel only (CFIUS commitment).
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Regulations to prevent foreign data access, with due diligence and reporting requirements.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The new rule introduces additional due diligence and reporting requirements that are not currently covered by the baseline policy, potentially requiring enhanced monitoring and reporting mechanisms.

remediations.title: Enhance Data Access Monitoring and Reporting
remediations.description: Implement enhanced monitoring and reporting mechanisms to comply with the DOJ's due diligence and reporting requirements. This includes developing a system to track and report access to US user data by foreign entities.
acceptance_criteria: Implementation of a monitoring system for foreign data access
Development of a reporting mechanism for compliance with DOJ requirements

business_impact: Failure to comply with the DOJ's Final Rule could result in significant legal and financial penalties, as well as reputational damage, due to the sensitive nature of the data involved.
recommended_actions: Conduct a detailed gap analysis to identify specific compliance requirements under the new rule.
Develop and implement enhanced monitoring and reporting systems for data access.
Engage with legal and compliance teams to ensure alignment with DOJ requirements.
